findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina centered at the top of the page. The HTML rendering does not include this seal image.

findings.description: Header layout differs - PDF has left-aligned official names with centered seal, HTML has a different arrangement
findings.evidence: PDF shows ROY COOPER/Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER/Secretary, MICHAEL ABRACZINSKAS/Director on the left with the seal centered and NORTH CAROLINA/Environmental Quality below the seal. HTML arranges these differently.

findings.description: The DEQ logo in the footer is present but may differ slightly in placement
findings.evidence: PDF shows DEQ logo at bottom left with contact info to its right. HTML similarly shows the logo area but with different styling.

findings.description: Footer layout reorganized - contact information placement differs
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 has the DEQ footer at the bottom with logo and contact info side by side. HTML places this as a header-style element at the top of the page section with right-aligned contact info.

findings.description: Appendix list formatting differs slightly - PDF uses tabular alignment with colons while HTML uses similar formatting
findings.evidence: PDF has appendix labels (B-3, F-1, etc.) aligned with descriptions using tab stops. HTML preserves the same content but with slightly different alignment.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of Michael A. Abraczinskas is missing or not rendered in the final HTML page
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a handwritten signature above 'Michael A. Abraczinskas, Director'. The HTML rendering does not appear to include this signature image.

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 heading at all. The document lacks any heading hierarchy — there are no h1, h2, h3, or other heading elements used to structure the content. The entire document is presented as paragraphs and lists without any semantic heading structure.
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains no

,

, or other heading tags. The subject line and other structural elements are rendered as plain

tags.

findings.description: The page header information from page 1 (ROY COOPER, Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER, Secretary, MICHAEL ABRACZINSKAS, Director) is rendered both in a page-header div AND embedded within the scanned image, effectively duplicating the letterhead content. Additionally, the entire page 1 content appears both as part of the first image's context and then repeated as HTML text, creating a structural duplication pattern across all three content pages.
findings.evidence: Each page has both a

findings.description: The HTML uses multiple nested <main> elements (one outer wrapping the entire document, and additional <main> elements for each page's content), which is invalid HTML structure but does not result in missing content.
findings.evidence: There is an outer <main> tag, and then three additional <main> tags for each page's content section.

findings.description: Minor text variation: The PDF shows 'record.keeping' (likely a scanning artifact with a period instead of no space), while the HTML correctly renders it as 'recordkeeping'. This is an acceptable correction.

Alt text for images is generic rather than descriptive of specific content. The alt text for page 4's blank page image says 'Blank scanned page 4' which is accurate. The other alt texts are reasonable but generic.
findings.evidence: alt='Scanned letter from North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality, page 1', alt='Scanned letter from North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality, page 2', alt='Scanned letter from North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality, page 3 with signature', alt='Blank scanned page 4'
